report_name: Ransomware Civil War: KryBit RaaS Hacks and Leaks Rival Gang 0APT

threat_actors.name: KryBit
threat_actors.description: An emerging ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that retaliated against a rival gang, 0APT, by hacking and leaking their entire operational infrastructure. KryBit is actively conducting its own double-extortion ransomware campaign, having listed over 20 victims.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and establishing dominance in the cybercrime ecosystem.

threat_actors.name: 0APT
threat_actors.description: An amateur ransomware group that was hacked and dismantled by the rival KryBit gang. The group was exposed for fabricating victim claims and running its data leak site from an Android device.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion and building a reputation in the cybercrime world.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The KryBit group claims to exfiltrate sensitive data from victims before encryption as part of its double extortion tactic.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: KryBit likely used this technique to breach 0APT's amateur server setup, which was hosted on an Android device.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The KRYBIT ransomware encrypts files on victim networks to render them inaccessible and extort a ransom.

malware.name: KRYBIT ransomware
malware.description: A ransomware payload used by the KryBit RaaS group. It encrypts files, appends the .KRYBIT extension, and drops a ransom note named RECOVER-README.txt.

tools.name: AnLinux-ParrotOS
tools.description: A Linux environment for Android used by the 0APT group to host their data leak site, demonstrating their amateur operational setup.

tools.name: Tor
tools.description: Used by the KryBit group to host their data leak site for double extortion tactics.

infrastructure.name: 0APT Leak Site
infrastructure.description: An amateur data leak site hosted on an Android device running a Linux environment, used by the 0APT group before being dismantled by KryBit.

infrastructure.name: KryBit Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A Tor-based data leak site used by the KryBit group to publish exfiltrated victim data as part of their double extortion strategy.

indicators.name: KryBit Ransom Note File Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: KryBit Ransom Note Creation
id: 7c5a1b9e-8d3f-4e2a-9b0c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the KryBit ransomware note 'RECOVER-README.txt'.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/krybit-retaliates-against-0apt-with-extensive-data-leak
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-01
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\RECOVER-README.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the ransom note file dropped by KRYBIT ransomware.

indicators.name: KryBit Ransomware File Encryption
indicators.pattern:
title: KryBit Ransomware File Encryption Extension
id: 8b4d2c8f-9e2a-4d1b-8a9b-2c3d4e5f6a7b
status: experimental
description: Detects files being created with the .KRYBIT extension, indicative of KryBit ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/krybit-retaliates-against-0apt-with-extensive-data-leak
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-01
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.KRYBIT'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being renamed with the .KRYBIT extension, a key indicator of KRYBIT ransomware encryption.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9a3c1b2d-0f9e-4c8b-b8a9-3d4e5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/krybit-retaliates-against-0apt-with-extensive-data-leak
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-01
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used to delete volume shadow copies, a behavior typical of ransomware like KryBit to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Tor Network Traffic Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Tor Network Connection
id: a0b1c2d3-e4f5-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to common Tor directory or relay ports, which could indicate C2 or data exfiltration activity by groups like KryBit.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/krybit-retaliates-against-0apt-with-extensive-data-leak
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-01
tags:
  - attack.t1090
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort:
      - 9001
      - 9030
      - 9050
      - 9150
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to common Tor ports, which could indicate communication with KryBit's leak site or C2 infrastructure.
